staff.name: Marcus Johnson
staff.bio: Lived experience service-user involvement working alongside clinical treatment is undoubtedly the most important ingredient for the optimum outcome in the battle against addiction. I have been in recovery from alcoholism for over 10 years.

staff.name: Jason Spreckley
staff.bio: Until you become inspired by someone else, the change can't happen. I've been with SUIT for over 12 years. I have lived experience of addiction and I value peer-support.

staff.name: Michelle Lane
staff.bio: I went from feeling like an outsider, to someone who had found their tribe. The value of understanding that you get from someone who has experienced similar issues cannot be underestimated.

staff.name: Fallon Burnett
staff.role: Project Support Worker & Shared Care Lead
staff.bio: I am a champion of diversity, and want to break down barriers, remove societal stigma, and bring the community together.

staff.name: Christiane Jenkins
staff.bio: Appreciate connections & social interactions… we are not alone in our emotions. I'm 9 years into recovery. Using art for support in recovery introduces vibrant and productive networks, environments, and opportunities.

staff.name: Cody Jenkins
staff.bio: I work as SUIT's Data Illustrator. A family member went through their recovery with SUIT so I saw firsthand the support given.

staff.name: Vijay Sahota
staff.bio: Being part of the SUIT family gives me purpose, and I can use my lived experience of addiction to show others that recovery and change is possible.

staff.name: Matt Whittall
staff.role: Peer Support Worker - Hospital Drug & Alcohol Liaison Team
staff.bio: It takes real bravery to seek help. Be brave and reach out and you will find a team that will offer patience, calm, and a duty of care.

staff.name: Warren Sutherland
staff.role: SMART & Lived Experience Day Hab Coordinator
staff.bio: I hope you appreciate the words, but more than that, I hope it makes you think. My addiction wasn't wasted time — it was my lesson getting me ready to help others.

staff.name: Karolina Sowinska
staff.bio: Everyone deserves to have supporters on their side, to keep believing in them, to help them achieve. To keep being different, unique, valued. To survive.

staff.name: Kiran Kaur
staff.bio: Supporting the SUIT team and our community with administrative coordination.

volunteers.name: Giles Staddon
volunteers.bio: Providing vital office and wraparound support as a lived experience volunteer with SUIT.

volunteers.name: Richard Felton
volunteers.bio: Supporting clients and the SUIT office team through lived experience volunteering.

volunteers.name: Amy Felton
volunteers.bio: Providing wraparound support to clients at Paycare House through the Recovery Ambassador Programme.

volunteers.name: Ingrid Forman
volunteers.bio: Volunteering at SUIT, offering guidance and support to people in recovery.

volunteers.name: Salgram Dass
volunteers.bio: Supporting SUIT's cultural engagement work, helping to reach communities with language and cultural understanding.

volunteers.name: Dal Rai Singh
volunteers.bio: Volunteering with the Cultural Engagement Team to break down barriers in Punjabi-speaking communities.

volunteers.name: Jas Sallon Singh
volunteers.bio: Part of SUIT's Cultural Engagement Team, building trust and understanding in the community.

volunteers.name: Lynette John
volunteers.bio: Supporting SUIT's cultural engagement outreach and community events.

volunteers.name: Sarah Millington
volunteers.bio: Facilitating recovery groups at SUIT, using lived experience to support others.

volunteers.name: Mark Harris
volunteers.bio: Leading and supporting recovery group sessions as a lived experience volunteer.

volunteers.name: Barry Thompson
volunteers.bio: Facilitating groups and providing peer support to SUIT service users.

volunteers.name: Andrew Edwards
volunteers.bio: Supporting group sessions and recovery activities at SUIT through volunteering.

volunteers.name: Howie Atkin
volunteers.bio: Volunteering as a group facilitator, helping to create safe spaces for recovery discussions.

volunteers.name: Cordula Roosa'riedener
volunteers.bio: Facilitating recovery groups with compassion and lived experience knowledge.

volunteers.name: Chelsea Willetts
volunteers.bio: Supporting individuals within the criminal justice system through lived experience peer mentoring.

volunteers.name: Chloe Hughes
volunteers.bio: Studying Criminology at the University of Wolverhampton and volunteering with SUIT as a Research Assistant, contributing to evidence-based recovery research.
